Related: ENG-3421 implements the policy schema; SEC-78 is the security review. Links: linear/ENG-3421, security/SEC-78. Notes: No public API changes. Operators will need to declare kms.bindings for existing namespaces or opt into a grace period to avoid sudden denials.

source.title: Add KMS metrics + tracing spans for standardized wrapper calls
source.author: Nadia Rahman
source.description: Summary: Instruments all KMS operations invoked via the new shared wrapper with metrics and trace spans following Redwood telemetry conventions. Adds latency histograms, request/error counters (tagged by canonical error taxonomy), and a consistent tracing span shape so on-call can correlate KMS degradation to elevated 5xx/latency. Motivation: As we harden KMS integration paths (ENG-4821), we need consistent KMS dependency signals across control-plane and data-plane components. Recent support cases and a throttling-related incident (SUP-19377) showed we lacked enough granularity to quickly answer: (1) which operation is failing, (2) whether it’s retry amplification, (3) what error class dominates, and (4) which region/provider is impacted. This PR builds on pr-18421’s wrapper API and wires metrics/spans at the wrapper boundary (single place, consistent tags). What’s included: Metrics (emitted on every wrapper call): 1) redwood.kms.request.count (counter) tags: operation, provider, region, result (success|error), error_category (when error). 2) redwood.kms.request.latency_ms (histogram) tags: operation, provider, region, result. 3) redwood.kms.retries.count (counter) tags: operation, provider, region, error_category. 4) redwood.kms.throttled.count (counter) tags: provider, region (incremented for throttling-class responses, including SDK-specific throttle codes mapped into the canonical taxonomy). Tracing: Adds a span around each KMS wrapper call: span name: kms.<operation> (e.g., kms.decrypt, kms.encrypt, kms.generate_data_key, kms.describe_key) attributes: kms.operation, kms.provider, cloud.region, kms.key_ref (redacted; alias name when present, otherwise stable hash), kms.retry_attempt (per attempt), kms.error_category (when error), kms.http_status (when available from SDK metadata), and kms.request_id (when provided by provider SDK). Notes: we explicitly do not attach raw key ARNs or plaintext key IDs; key references are sanitized. Error taxonomy mapping: Uses the canonical categories discussed in ENG-4822 and the eng-security thread (AccessDenied, NotFound, Throttling, Timeout, InvalidCiphertext, DependencyUnavailable, Unknown). Mapping is implemented in kms/telemetry/error_mapping.go and shared by metrics + span attributes + logs. Sampling: Spans respect existing tracing sampling decisions (no new always-on sampling); metrics are always emitted. This is intentional so we can alert on dependency health even when traces are sampled. Changed areas (high level): - pkg/security/kms/wrapper/*: inject TelemetryReporter into wrapper client; emit metrics + spans; ensure metrics are recorded for success/failure and for each attempt where relevant. - pkg/telemetry/metrics/registry.go: registers KMS meters and canonical tag keys (prevents ad-hoc tag drift). - pkg/telemetry/tracing/spans.go: helper to build KMS spans with consistent attribute names and safe key redaction. - pkg/security/kms/providers/aws.go, gcp.go, azure.go: normalizes provider-specific error codes into canonical error categories; plumbs request-id and status metadata when available. - docs/internal/observability/metrics.md: adds KMS metric names + tag glossary (internal only). Example metric/tag conventions (for reviewers): - operation values: decrypt | encrypt | generate_data_key | describe_key | reencrypt - provider values: aws | gcp | azure - error_category values: AccessDenied | NotFound | Throttling | Timeout | InvalidCiphertext | DependencyUnavailable | Unknown - region values: us-east-1, etc. (or provider equivalent) Testing: - Unit tests for error mapping across providers (golden table tests). - Unit tests verifying tag sets (ensures we don’t regress into high-cardinality tags). - Local smoke: ran data-plane integration tests against the KMS stub; verified metrics emission via in-process exporter. Rollout / follow-ups: - SRE will add dashboards/alerts in a separate PR (observability-pack/pr-844). - Once wrapper migration is complete across remaining call sites, we can remove legacy ad-hoc KMS logging in services. Checklist: - [x] No sensitive data in metrics/tags (no key ARNs, no account IDs) - [x] Error taxonomy matches ENG-4822 - [x] Metrics follow Redwood naming + tagging conventions - [x] Traces use consistent span naming/attributes - [x] Unit tests for mapping and tag set validation
commits: kms: add telemetry reporter interface + default implementation
kms: emit request counters + latency histograms at wrapper boundary
kms: add error taxonomy mapping for aws/gcp/azure SDK errors
tracing: add kms.<operation> spans with safe attributes
tests: golden mapping + tag set validation
source.review_activity: Allison Grant (requested changes): Please align metric tag keys with existing conventions; avoid key_id as a tag; add explicit note about high-cardinality prevention. Nadia Rahman (author): Updated registry to centralize allowed tags; renamed tag from key_id -> key_ref (sanitized alias/hash); added a unit test asserting no raw ARNs; expanded docs/internal/observability/metrics.md with cardinality guidelines. Kevin Osei: Looks good; please ensure error_category is present only when result=error and that success paths don’t emit empty/unknown values. Nadia: Adjusted instrumentation to omit error_category on success; added test. Sean Gallagher: Can we also emit a retries counter so we can spot retry storms early? Nadia: Added redwood.kms.retries.count tagged by operation/provider/region/error_category. Ethan Park: Confirmed wrapper boundary is correct place; thanks for not leaking ARNs. Caleb Johnson: Span naming/attributes match tracing guidelines; approved. Final approvals: Allison Grant, Kevin Osei, Sean Gallagher, Caleb Johnson. Merged via squash.

source.title: Cost‑Smarts: telemetry-driven prefill scheduler and quantization hint adapter
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Adds a telemetry-driven prefill scheduler and a lightweight quantization hint adapter. Improves unit economics by ~7-12% on mixed workloads; configurable defaults can be pinned. No API changes to public inference endpoints.
source.body:
Context and motivation: Large parts of our cost-automation stack rely on static prefill and quantization defaults that are tuned per-model but not per-workload. Customers running mixed telemetry (short chat bursts + long completion bursts) see suboptimal unit economics because prefill and quant decisions are not being informed by runtime signals. This PR introduces 'Cost‑Smarts' — a telemetry-driven prefill scheduler plus a quantization hint adapter that surfaces unit-econ deltas into the serving runtime policy engine.

What this change does: Implements a small, runtime-side scheduler that consumes existing request telemetry (sequence length histograms, prefix-kv hit ratios, tail-latency percentile) and emits short-lived prefill decisions (aggressive/standard/disabled) for a given route+model tuple. In parallel, the quantization hint adapter exposes a per-route score representing the estimated per-token cost uplift vs quality risk, which the router can use to select a compatible quantized variant when under cost pressure. Both components default to conservative behavior and are configurable from the runtime config and via the console.

Design highlights and tradeoffs: The scheduler is intentionally lightweight — it aggregates telemetry in 10s windows and uses rule-based heuristics derived from recent econ experiments (prefill when median prefix length > 16 and kv-hit < 0.6, or when 95p latency < configured threshold). We intentionally avoid ML models for initial rollout to keep behavior explainable and safe. The quant hint adapter computes a rollout-friendly score using a small lookup table based on model family, qprofile, and observed batch sizes; the score is a float in [0.0, 1.0] representing 'cost-favorability'.

Checklist: - [x] Runtime scheduler + config knobs - [x] Quant hint adapter + API contract with router - [x] Console defaults/UI flag (defaults panel) - [x] End-to-end unit and integration tests - [x] Telemetry metrics + dashboards - [x] Docs and migration notes

Files and high-level changes: Added a new runtime module runtime/prefill_scheduler.rs implementing the aggregator and decision API; runtime/quant_hint_adapter.rs implements the lightweight scoring; modified router/policy_router.go to accept quant_hint scores when selecting a variant; console defaults UI tweak in console/src/routes/costDefaults; new telemetry metrics in observability/metrics.yaml and a Grafana dashboard JSON in observability/dashboards/prefill_cost_smarts.json. Tests added under runtime/tests and router/integration.

Safety and rollout: Defaults are conservative. The system starts in passive mode (observing only) unless enabled by a new env var PREFILL_SCHEDULER_MODE=active. New config 'costSmarts' allows per-tenant opt-in and pinning of decisions. We added automatic fallback to existing prefill/quant defaults in all code paths.

Performance and unit economics: Internal benchmarks on a mixed workload trace (chat + long completions) show a 7–12% reduction in per-token GPU seconds when cost-smarts is active + quant variants available. Tail latency impact was within measurement noise in 95th percentile tests when prefill choices leaned conservative.

Migration notes: Operators can enable cost-smarts per-tenant via the console or config. No changes needed to existing customer configs unless they want to opt in to aggressive prefill or quant-favoring behavior.

Related work: complements recent econ autotuners (ENG-2201) and the prefill-prober; differs by being runtime-native and telemetry-driven rather than offline profile-based.

How to review: Start with runtime/prefill_scheduler.rs for decision logic, then router/policy_router.go for selection changes. Console changes are small and behind a feature flag. Tests include unit coverage for heuristics and an integration scenario that replays a production trace.

Summary: Implements a safe, incremental path to using live telemetry to improve prefill/quant decisions and lower per-token cost without changing public inference APIs.
commits: a1b2c3d: introduce prefill scheduler module and basic aggregator
b2c3d4e: add quant_hint_adapter and scoring LUT
c3d4e5f: wire quant_hint into policy router selection logic
d4e5f6a: console defaults UI and feature flag
e5f6a7b: telemetry metrics + dashboards + tests
f6a7b8c: integration tests and docs
review_comments: Ethan Morales (2025-10-13): Can we make the 10s aggregation window configurable? Some tenants have higher burstiness.
Priya Natarajan (2025-10-13): Added PREFILL_AGG_WINDOW in config and exposed as console slider; default remains 10s.
Sofia Klein (2025-10-14): Router change looks good but please add a guard to prevent selection of quant variants for routes with explicit 'quality: strict' metadata.
Priya Natarajan (2025-10-15): Added guard in policy_router.go; added unit tests to cover strict-quality routes.
Marcus Li (2025-10-16): CI flaked on perf-bench; re-ran and results look stable. Recommend adding a perf smoke gate before enabling aggressive mode.
Priya Natarajan (2025-10-16): Added perf_smoke_gate config and console warning when enabling aggressive mode.
conversation_summary: Initial design reviewed by runtime team; focus on explainability and safe defaults.
Minor API surface change in router limited to internal policy input; no external API changes.
Decided to keep heuristics rule-based for first rollout; follow-up task (ENG-2240) to experiment with learned policies.
Feature gated behind per-tenant opt-in and an environment flag for quick rollback.
source.summary: Merged telemetry-driven prefill scheduler and quantization hint adapter to reduce per-token cost with safe defaults and per-tenant opt-in. Includes runtime + router + console + telemetry + tests.
source.notes_for_ops: Enable via PREFILL_SCHEDULER_MODE=active and set costSmarts.enabled in tenant config. Monitor prefillscheduler.prefill_decisions and quant_hint.score metrics. If regressions appear, set PREFILL_SCHEDULER_MODE=passive to return to observation-only mode.

source.title: Dedicated autoscaler: add metrics + structured logs for desired vs bounded capacity and prediction error
source.author: Logan Wright
source.description:
Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired replicas vs bounded desired replicas, (2) bound-hit counters (clamped to min/max), (3) prediction error and staleness when predictive mode is enabled, and (4) an explicit scale-event reason enum that shows up in both metrics and logs.

What’s in this PR:
- New gauge metrics (emitted every autoscaler tick per deployment/region):
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.desired_replicas (the raw desired output from the decision function)
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bounded_desired_replicas (after applying min/max + safety floors)
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.current_replicas
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.queue_depth (existing signal re-emitted consistently for dashboards)
- New counters:
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bound_hit_total{bound="min"|"max"} increments when desired is clamped
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.scale_event_total{direction="up"|"down", reason=*} increments on actual scale actions
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.prediction_stale_total increments when a forecast is ignored due to staleness
- New histograms (guarded by config to avoid extra cardinality):
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.prediction_error_ratio (|predicted - actual| / max(actual,1))
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.decision_latency_ms (internal time to compute desired)
- Structured decision logs (one per tick) with stable field names for searchability and compliance reviews. Key fields:
  - deployment_id, customer_id, region
  - policy_min, policy_max, predictive_enabled
  - desired_replicas, bounded_desired_replicas, current_replicas
  - scale_action (none|up|down), scale_delta
  - reason (enum), reason_detail (short string)
  - predictor_used (true/false), predictor_age_seconds

Scale reason enum (used consistently across metric label + log field):
- reactive_queue_depth
- reactive_saturation
- predictive_forecast
- bounded_by_max
- bounded_by_min
- cooldown_active
- no_change
- invalid_prediction

Cardinality / labeling:
- All metrics are labeled with deployment_id and region. customer_id is included only in structured logs (NOT as a metric label) to avoid high-cardinality blowups. This matches the current fleet metrics policy.
- reason labels are limited to the enum above (no freeform strings).

Illustrative example (structured log):
{"msg":"autoscaler_tick","deployment_id":"ded-9d2f...","region":"us-east-1","policy_min":4,"policy_max":24,"predictive_enabled":true,"desired_replicas":31,"bounded_desired_replicas":24,"current_replicas":20,"scale_action":"up","scale_delta":4,"reason":"bounded_by_max","reason_detail":"desired=31 clamped to max=24","predictor_used":true,"predictor_age_seconds":58}

Tests:
- Unit tests for reason selection (predictive vs reactive overrides) to ensure we don’t emit conflicting reason labels.
- Integration-style test for clamp behavior to ensure bound_hit_total increments exactly once per tick when clamping occurs.

Follow-ups (out of this PR):
- Grafana dashboard panels + alert rules live in observability-pack PRs.
- Control plane audit event schema (who/when/what) is handled in the audit-events PR.

Rollout:
- Metrics are behind the existing dedicated_autoscaling_bounds feature gate. When disabled, no new bounded/predictive metrics are emitted.
- Prediction histograms default off in prod until we validate cost/volume during pilot (config: autoscaler.emit_prediction_histograms=false).

Refs: ENG-22143, ENG-22140. Also informs debugging for SUP-28479 (pilot oscillation) by exposing scale_event reasons and predictor staleness.
commits: autoscaler: emit desired vs bounded desired gauges
autoscaler: add bound_hit_total + scale_event_total counters
predictive: emit prediction error + staleness metrics (guarded)
logging: structured autoscaler tick log + reason enum
tests: reason selection + clamp counter behavior
chore: metric docs + lint fixes
source.conversation:
Allison Grant (review): Can we avoid customer_id as a metric label? We’ve been burned by that before.
Logan Wright (author): Yep — removed customer_id from metric labels; keeping it only in structured logs. Metrics now label on {deployment_id, region} only.

Vanessa Ortiz (review): Reason strings need to be stable; please make it an enum and keep it aligned between logs + metrics.
Logan Wright (author): Done. Added reasons.go with a typed enum + a single mapping used by both emission paths. Added tests to prevent accidental freeform reasons.

Sergio Costa (review): Histogram buckets for prediction_error_ratio look too granular; also can we gate histograms behind config? We might not want that volume on every tick.
Logan Wright (author): Added config flag autoscaler.emit_prediction_histograms=false by default in prod config; updated buckets to {0.05,0.1,0.2,0.5,1,2}. Gauges/counters stay on.

Rafael Mendes (review): For on-call, we need to quickly tell if we’re clamped by max vs cooldown. Can we ensure those show up as distinct reasons and not get overwritten by reactive triggers?
Logan Wright (author): Updated precedence: if action is prevented by cooldown, reason=cooldown_active. If action happens but desired was clamped, we use reason=bounded_by_max/min with reason_detail preserving the upstream trigger. Added test coverage.

Jordan Lee (review): Please keep reason_detail short and avoid embedding full JSON blobs.
Logan Wright (author): Agreed. reason_detail is now capped to 160 chars and uses a fixed template.

Approvals: Allison Grant approved; Vanessa Ortiz approved; Rafael Mendes approved.

Merge: Squashed and merged after green CI.
source.release_notes: Internal: Dedicated autoscaler now emits desired vs bounded desired capacity metrics, bound-hit counters, and structured scale-event reasons; predictive mode also emits forecast error/staleness metrics when enabled.

title: Dedicated autoscaler: add metrics + structured logs for desired vs bounded capacity and prediction error
preview:
Context: As part of Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode, we need auditability and operability around what the autoscaler *wanted* to do vs what it actually did after applying customer bounds (min/max) and safety invariants. This PR adds first-class metrics and structured logs for (1) requested desired replicas vs bounded desired replicas, (2) bound-hit counters (clamped to min/max), (3) prediction error and staleness when predictive mode is enabled, and (4) an explicit scale-event reason enum that shows up in both metrics and logs.

What’s in this PR:
- New gauge metrics (emitted every autoscaler tick per deployment/region):
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.desired_replicas (the raw desired output from the decision function)
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bounded_desired_replicas (after applying min/max + safety floors)
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.current_replicas
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.queue_depth (existing signal re-emitted consistently for dashboards)
- New counters:
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.bound_hit_total{bound="min"|"max"} increments when desired is clamped
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.scale_event_total{direction="up"|"down", reason=*} increments on actual scale actions
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.prediction_stale_total increments when a forecast is ignored due to staleness
- New histograms (guarded by config to avoid extra cardinality):
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.prediction_error_ratio (|predicted - actual| / max(actual,1))
  - redwood.dedicated.autoscaler.decision_latency_ms (internal time to compute desired)
- Structured decision logs (one per tick) with stable field names for searchability and compliance reviews. Key fields:
  - deployment_id, customer_id, region
  - policy_min, policy_max, predictive_enabled
  - desired_replicas, bounded_desired_replicas, current_replicas
  - scale_action (none|up|down), scale_delta
  - reason (enum), reason_detail (short string)
  - predictor_used (true/false), predictor_age_seconds

Scale reason enum (used consistently across metric label + log field):
- reactive_queue_depth
- reactive_saturation
- predictive_forecast
- bounded_by_max
- bounded_by_min
- cooldown_active
- no_change
- invalid_prediction

Cardinality / labeling:
- All metrics are labeled with deployment_id and region. customer_id is included only in structured logs (NOT as a metric label) to avoid high-cardinality blowups. This matches the current fleet metrics policy.
- reason labels are limited to the enum above (no freeform strings).

Illustrative example (structured log):
{"msg":"autoscaler_tick","deployment_id":"ded-9d2f...","region":"us-east-1","policy_min":4,"policy_max":24,"predictive_enabled":true,"desired_replicas":31,"bounded_desired_replicas":24,"current_replicas":20,"scale_action":"up","scale_delta":4,"reason":"bounded_by_max","reason_detail":"desired=31 clamped to max=24","predictor_used":true,"predictor_age_seconds":58}

Tests:
- Unit tests for reason selection (predictive vs reactive overrides) to ensure we don’t emit conflicting reason labels.
- Integration-style test for clamp behavior to ensure bound_hit_total increments exactly once per tick when clamping occurs.

Follow-ups (out of this PR):
- Grafana dashboard panels + alert rules live in observability-pack PRs.
- Control plane audit event schema (who/when/what) is handled in the audit-events PR.

Rollout:
- Metrics are behind the existing dedicated_autoscaling_bounds feature gate. When disabled, no new bounded/predictive metrics are emitted.
- Prediction histograms default off in prod until we validate cost/volume during pilot (config: autoscaler.emit_prediction_histograms=false).

Refs: ENG-22143, ENG-22140. Also informs debugging for SUP-28479 (pilot oscillation) by exposing scale_event reasons and predictor staleness.
